text:
list 6 interesting spots in Copenhagen as the potential destinations, as well as the reason for choosing them, the admission prices, and the opening days, and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.

To better evaluate the result, please organize your answers according to the following format:

Example:
	Location 1:
	location: UCLA
	reason: it's a famous university
	price: free
	opening days: all days
Location 2:
	...
Tips:
	1. Parking fee is high
It is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
The Round Tower is a 17th-century tower located in central Copenhagen. It was built as an astronomical observatory.
Noma is a world-famous restaurant known for its culinary masterpieces. A dinner here is a must for any food lover.
Known as The Marble Church for its rococo architecture, Frederiks Kirke is an iconic symbol of Copenhagen's architectural heritage.
This statue is a character from Grimm's Fairy Tales and is one of the most popular tourist attractions in Copenhagen.
Amarienborg is the winter home of the Danish royal family. It's a must-visit due to its historical significance and the change of guard that happens at 12:00 every day.
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen. Don't worry about language barriers.
Copenhagen follows left-hand drive. Be careful if you plan to drive or rent a car.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius. Make sure to pack warm clothes if you're visiting in winter.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so make sure to exchange your money.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. Don't forget to bring an adapter if necessary.
